Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1) Order Compelling Discovery Responses; 2) Order Compelling Attendance And Testimony At Deposition; 3) Order That Pltfs' Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; And 4) Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Hunter Pyle/Jorge Aguilar, II, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and moving party: Juan Avila
John Henning, III, Esq., appeared for defendant and opposing party: SEIU Local 87

RULING:
HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: Plaintiffs Juan Avila, Juan Calderon, Erick Manzanarez, Jacqueline Ramirez, Maria Vivanco's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Compel Attendance And Testimony At Deposition, For Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond to Plaintiffs' first sets of Requests For Production Of Documents And Things, Special and Form Interrogatories within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiffs are further ordered to attend attendance and testimony at deposition at a time and place to be noticed by Plaintiffs. The court further orders that the matters specified in Plaintiffs' Request For Admissions be deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,100, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

MODIFICATION:
- Motion denied as it seeks to have request for admissions be deemed admitted, responses have been served.
- Respond within 20 days of notice of order.
COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
